title: Design System: Light it Up — Multi-Accent (MLB / NHL / NFL / NBA)

tokenMechanism.description: Semantic CSS tokens on :root — --accent, --accent-wash, --accent-line, and --ramp-0..--ramp-4 — are overridden by :root[data-league='nhl'|'nfl'|'nba']. Default :root = MLB green. The selected league is set on document.documentElement[dataset.league] and deep-linked via the URL hash. Neutral tokens (field, slate, ink) are never overridden and stay shared across leagues.
tokenMechanism.colorMixGlows: Per-cell bulb glows are derived from the ramp via color-mix(): e.g. box-shadow: 0 0 5px -1px color-mix(in srgb, var(--ramp-1) 55%, transparent). No hard-coded glow color exists — everything inherits from the active league.

display.purpose: The masthead 'Light it Up' only — Archivo Black, signage scale.

title.purpose: Big stat values; Hanken Grotesk 700 with tabular figures.

body.purpose: Subhead and prose; Hanken Grotesk 400, measure ~52ch.

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked micro-text: stat labels, tag, search label.

shadows.name: bulb-glow-1
shadows.purpose: Emitted light on a low-value played cell; inherits active league accent.

shadows.name: bulb-glow-4
shadows.purpose: Emitted light on the peak-value cell; inherits active league accent.

shadows.name: bulb-glow-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover bloom on the highest-value played cell; inherits active league accent.

shadows.name: focus-halo
shadows.purpose: Soft accent halo on the focused search field; follows active league.

motion.name: reveal-step
motion.purpose: Per-cell stagger for the 1→N ignite sweep.

motion.name: reveal-cap
motion.purpose: Hard ceiling on the total ignite sweep, even for 162 slots.

components.name: League Switcher
components.description: A segmented tab control in the masthead (role='tablist', four .league-tab buttons). Each tab carries its own league accent via --tab; all four hues are visible simultaneously. The active tab is filled solid in its accent with dark ink text. Clicking or arrow-keying sets data-league on :root, updates aria-selected, deep-links via URL hash, and instantly recolors the entire theme (all --accent* and --ramp-* tokens). Under prefers-reduced-motion, all transitions are instant. Dynamic copy (subhead, legend labels, page title, search aria-label) updates on switch.
components.html: <nav class="league-switch" role="tablist" aria-label="Choose a league"><button class="league-tab" role="tab" type="button" data-league-id="mlb" aria-selected="true">MLB</button><button class="league-tab" role="tab" type="button" data-league-id="nhl" aria-selected="false">NHL</button><button class="league-tab" role="tab" type="button" data-league-id="nfl" aria-selected="false">NFL</button><button class="league-tab" role="tab" type="button" data-league-id="nba" aria-selected="false">NBA</button></nav>

components.name: Heatmap Cell
components.description: The signature bulb. One slot per team game (or per quarter for NFL): hollow socket = not yet played, slate = did not play, ignited by luminance when played. Glow derived from active league ramp via color-mix().
components.css: .ds-cell{width:17px;height:17px;border-radius:4px;background:#0e151b;box-shadow:inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,.025);transition:background .4s cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1),box-shadow .4s cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1),transform .14s cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1)}.ds-cell[data-state='missed']{background:#283440;box-shadow:none}.ds-cell[data-state='played'][data-level='0']{background:var(--ramp-0,#17241d);box-shadow:inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,.045)}.ds-cell[data-state='played'][data-level='1']{background:var(--ramp-1,#2f7d4a);box-shadow:0 0 5px -1px color-mix(in srgb,var(--ramp-1,#2f7d4a) 55%,transparent)}.ds-cell[data-state='played'][data-level='2']{background:var(--ramp-2,#39c463);box-shadow:0 0 7px -1px color-mix(in srgb,var(--ramp-2,#39c463) 60%,transparent)}.ds-cell[data-state='played'][data-level='3']{background:var(--ramp-3,#5be684);box-shadow:0 0 10px -1px color-mix(in srgb,var(--ramp-3,#5be684) 65%,transparent)}.ds-cell[data-state='played'][data-level='4']{background:var(--ramp-4,#9dffba);box-shadow:0 0 14px -1px color-mix(in srgb,var(--ramp-4,#9dffba) 80%,transparent)}.ds-cell[data-state='played']{cursor:pointer}.ds-cell[data-state='played']:hover,.ds-cell[data-state='played']:focus-visible{transform:translateY(-1px) scale(1.18);outline:none;box-shadow:0 0 18px 2px var(--ramp-4,#9dffba)}

components.name: Search Field
components.description: Slate field with a leading glyph that turns the active league accent on focus, plus a soft accent halo. The aria-label is dynamic per league.

components.name: Suggestion Row
components.description: A flush listbox row; active/hover is an accent wash with the team abbrev turning accent.

components.name: Nameplate Stat
components.description: A scoreboard stat: accent tabular value over an uppercase faint label. Labels are dynamic per league (e.g. 'Total Bases' for MLB, 'Points' for NHL).

components.name: Tag
components.description: An accent-wash metadata pill (never a button).

components.name: Tooltip Chip
components.description: The broadcast data chip shown on a played cell: matchup in accent, game line in dim ink.

components.name: Alert
components.description: A warning-track error panel with a leading glowing dot. Never flashes.
components.html: No 2026 games for that player yet — check back after Opening Day.

narrative.overview: This is an incandescent stadium scoreboard after dark. The page is the black field at night; a player's season ignites across it as a panel of bulbs, each one's brightness scaled to the league's metric for a single game. The tool covers four leagues — MLB, NHL, NFL, and NBA — each lit in its own signal accent: phosphor green for MLB, magenta for NHL, blue for NFL, orange for NBA. The scoreboard is the same scoreboard every time; only the signal color and the metric it measures change. The whole system exists to make one thing — the heatmap — feel alive and worth looking at, the way a scoreboard does when the home team is hot. Energy comes from a single committed signal color per selected league and the way light reads off a dark field, not from ornament. Everything that isn't the board stays quiet so the board can shout. The system explicitly rejects the generic SaaS dashboard (gradient-card grids, the hero-metric template, cookie-cutter admin chrome) and the cluttered stat sheet (ESPN / Baseball-Reference table overload). It is also a deliberate move away from its own origin: a GitHub-contributions grid in default greens on white. Here the grid is reframed as a stadium board — warm, emissive, and dark.
keyCharacteristics: One hero (the heatmap), one signal color per selected league, one dark field.
Intensity is carried by luminance, never hue alone — colorblind-safe by construction in all four league accents.
Athletic, signage-grade display type over disciplined, legible body type.
Light, not shadow: depth is tonal layering plus the glow a bulb emits.

rules.name: The Luminance Rule
rules.body: Every league's metric ramp is read by brightness, not hue. Every step is lighter than the last. Never encode the value of a game in hue alone — a red-green colorblind viewer must still read the board correctly. This holds for all four league accents; every ramp is luminance-monotonic and verified AA on Outfield Black.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: The accent is the only saturated color on the surface; which hue that is depends on the selected league. It means 'live / focused / this is the data.' If you find yourself reaching for the accent as decoration, stop — use a slate or an ink tone instead. The one prose exception is the metric name in the masthead subhead: the accent there names the metric the whole view is about, so it reads as signal, not decoration.

rules.name: The Three-State Rule
rules.body: Every slot is exactly one of: played (lit bulb on the metric ramp — tooltip + keyboard focus, including Ramp 0 for a zero-metric game), did not play (#283440 filled slate — hover tooltip, not focusable), or not yet played (#0e151b hollow socket — inert). The board lays out one slot per team game in schedule order (or per quarter for NFL). Reading the season as played / missed / upcoming is the point.

rules.name: The Scoreboard Weight Rule
rules.body: Archivo Black appears exactly twice — the masthead and the nameplate. Body, labels, and stats are always Hanken Grotesk. A page full of black weight is shouting, not designing.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Every number uses tabular figures. Scoreboards don't let digits wobble.

rules.name: The Light-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat and separated by tone. When you need depth, ask whether the element emits (a bulb → glow) or floats (a menu → drop shadow). Decorative ambient shadows on flat panels are forbidden.

dos: Do let the heatmap be the hero — give it the most room and quiet everything else.
Do carry game value with luminance, and pair color with a non-color signal (the tooltip's exact number).
Do keep the accent rare and meaningful (live data, focus, links) — the One Signal Rule.
Do reserve Archivo Black for the masthead and nameplate only.
Do convey depth with tone and emitted glow, not ambient drop shadows.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: the ignite sweep and league-switch recolor collapse to instant swaps.
Do keep AA contrast for all four league accents and keep every interactive slot keyboard-reachable with a visible focus ring.
Do use --accent, --accent-wash, --accent-line, and --ramp-* tokens everywhere so the theme mechanism works across all leagues.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard: no gradient-card grids, no hero-metric template, no admin-panel chrome.
Don't build a cluttered stat sheet: no ESPN / Baseball-Reference table overload.
Don't regress to the Tailwind-placeholder look (gray-on-white, default green focus ring, system fonts).
Don't use gradient text — solid ink only; emphasis via weight and size.
Don't encode a game's value in hue alone, and never give a future-game socket a tooltip, glow, or keyboard focus.
Don't add decorative glassmorphism or ambient shadows on flat panels.
Don't hard-code a league accent color — always use the CSS token vars so the multi-league theme mechanism works.
